items.title: Palm Garden AI develops Coherence Guard relational decision layer for human-facing robots
items.summary: Palm Garden AI is developing Coherence Guard, a platform-agnostic relational decision layer for human-facing robots, to enable them to behave appropriately around people. The technology evaluates candidate actions from a relational and contextual perspective before or during execution, considering factors such as timing, proximity, emotional tone, trust preservation, and respectful withdrawal. Coherence Guard aims to complement existing safety systems rather than replace them, supporting auditability, logging, scenario testing, and configurable thresholds for robots operating in human environments. The system is designed to be flexible, running on edge devices, premises, or clouds, with a licensed software layer offering optional SaaS components for configuration, simulation support, analytics, and updates.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Palm Garden AI develops Coherence Guard relational decision layer for human-facing robots." This breakthrough technology aims to enhance the safety and etiquette of robots interacting with humans by introducing a platform-agnostic relational decision layer. Palm Garden AI has developed Coherence Guard, which evaluates candidate actions from a relational and contextual perspective, considering factors such as timing, proximity, emotional tone, trust preservation, and respectful withdrawal. The system complements existing safety systems rather than replacing them, supporting auditability, logging, scenario testing, and configurable thresholds for robots operating in human environments. Coherence Guard is designed to be flexible, running on edge devices, premises, or clouds, with a licensed software layer offering optional SaaS components for configuration, simulation support, analytics, and updates. This innovation has the potential to significantly improve robot-human interactions and safety standards in various industries.

items.title: Faraday Future highlights robotics expansion and shipment milestone at Automate 2026
items.summary: Faraday Future's AI subsidiary, FFAI, is accelerating its commercial robotics expansion with the debut at Automate 2026. The company showcased a "one brain, multiple forms" robotics platform and announced progress toward first-half shipment targets, expecting June shipments to exceed 100 units and total deliveries to surpass 220 units ahead of schedule. FFAI presented humanoid robots, mobile manipulators, and education-focused systems built around an Embodied AI software platform, including the All-New Futurist humanoid robot priced at $89,900.
items.audio_script:
From Robotics & Automation News, "Faraday Future highlights robotics expansion and shipment milestone at Automate 2026". Faraday Future's AI subsidiary, FFAI, has marked a significant commercial robotics expansion milestone, showcasing its "one brain, multiple forms" robotics platform and announcing progress toward meeting first-half shipment targets. At the recent Automate 2026 event, FFAI presented a range of robots, including humanoid models, mobile manipulators, and education-focused systems built around an Embodied AI software platform. The company also announced that shipments are expected to exceed 100 units by June, with total deliveries anticipated to surpass 220 units ahead of schedule. FFAI's flagship product, the All-New Futurist humanoid robot priced at $89,900, was also showcased, highlighting the company's focus on commercial robotics innovation.

FFAI demonstrated its capabilities through the development of multiple robots and systems, leveraging its Embodied AI software platform as a core component. The company's approach allows for a "one brain, multiple forms" strategy, enabling FFAI to create diverse robotic solutions tailored to various industries and applications. By showcasing its range of products and announcing shipment milestones, FFAI has reinforced its position in the commercial robotics market.

The progress announced by FFAI at Automate 2026 suggests that the company is on track to meet its first-half shipment targets, indicating a successful commercial robotics expansion strategy. As the company continues to push forward with its commercial goals, it will be essential to monitor its future developments and product releases for continued growth and innovation in the industry.
